subcommands.name: undo
subcommands.description: rollback to a previous rollout

options.description: the length of time to wait before ending watch, zero means never. Any other values should contain a corresponding time unit (e.g. 1s, 2m, 3h)

subcommands.name: run
subcommands.description: create and run a particular image in a pod

options.description: annotations to apply to the pod
if true, wait for the Pod to start running, and then attach to the Pod as if 'kubectl attach ...' were called. Default false, unless '-i/--stdin' is set, in whi
must be "background", "orphan", or "foreground". Selects the deletion cascading strategy for the dependents (e.g. Pods created by a ReplicationController). Defa
if true and extra arguments are present, use them as the 'command' field in the container, rather than the 'args' field which is the default
environment variables to set in the container
if true, service is created for the container(s) which are run
if true, immediately remove resources from API and bypass graceful deletion. Note that immediate deletion of some resources may result in inconsistency or data
period of time in seconds given to the resource to terminate gracefully. Ignored if negative. Set to 1 for immediate shutdown. Can only be set to 0 when --force
the host port mapping for the container port. To demonstrate a single-machine container
the image for the container to run
the image pull policy for the container. If left empty, this value will not be specified by the client and defaulted by the server
comma separated labels to apply to the pod(s). Will override previous values
if the pod is started in interactive mode or with stdin, leave stdin open after the first attach completes. By default, stdin will be closed after the first att
the resource requirement limits for this container. For example, 'cpu=200m,memory=512Mi'. Note that server side components may assign limits depending on the se
an inline JSON override for the generated object. If this is non-empty, it is used to override the generated object. Requires that the object supply a valid api
the length of time (like 5s, 2m, or 3h, higher than zero) to wait until at least one pod is running
the port that this container exposes
if true, suppress prompt messages
the resource requirement requests for this container. For example, 'cpu=100m,memory=256Mi'. Note that server side components may assign requests depending on th
the restart policy for this Pod. Legal values [Always, OnFailure, Never]. If set to 'Always' a deployment is created, if set to 'OnFailure' a job is created, if
if true, delete resources created in this command for attached containers
if true, the configuration of current object will be saved in its annotation. Otherwise, the annotation will be unchanged. This flag is useful when you want to
service account to set in the pod spec
keep stdin open on the container(s) in the pod, even if nothing is attached
the length of time to wait before giving up on a delete, zero means determine a timeout from the size of the object
allocated a TTY for each container in the pod
if true, wait for resources to be gone before returning. This waits for finalizers

subcommands.name: wait
subcommands.description: experimental: Wait for a specific condition on one or many resources

options.description: select all resources in the namespace of the specified resource types
if present, list the requested object(s) across all namespaces. Namespace in current context is ignored even if specified with --namespace
the condition to wait on: [delete|condition=condition-name]
the length of time to wait before giving up. Zero means check once and don't wait, negative means wait for a week
